# terrible_community_postgresql_postgresql_alter_system (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this data source against
- `param` (String) Name of PostgreSQL server parameter.
- `value` (String) Parameter value to set. Specify the value in appropriate units! For memory-related parameters of type integer, it is `kB`, `MB`, `GB`, and `TB`. Use `_RESET` to run the `ALTER SYSTEM RESET param` which will remove a corresponding entry from `postgresql.auto.conf`. Always returns `changed=True`. For boolean parameters, pass the `"on"` or `"off"` string.

### Optional

- `login_db` (String) Name of database to connect.
- `session_role` (String) Switch to session_role after connecting. The specified session_role must be a role that the current login_user is a member of. Permissions checking for SQL commands is carried out as though the `session_role` were the one that had logged in originally.
- `trust_input` (Boolean) If `false`, check whether values of parameters are potentially dangerous. It makes sense to use `false` only when SQL injections are possible.
